title: Eastern Harees
description: Traditional Middle Eastern dish made with wheat and often served at breakfast or dinner. This recipe serves 4-6 people and is relatively easy to make.
instructions: Rinse the wheat and soak it in water for at least 4 hours or overnight.
Combine the soaked wheat, harees, spices, and salt in a large pot.
Gradually add the remaining 4 cups of water and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 1-2 hours or until the mixture thickens.
Strain the mixture and discard the solids.
Return the mixture to the pot and cook for another 30 minutes or until it reaches a porridge-like consistency.
Garnish with crunchy onions and serve hot.
